attacking_style: Avoids premature attacks, preferring to conserve energy and strike when opponents are exhausted. Times moves after others have depleted themselves rather than initiating early aggression.
team_tactics: Limited evidence of team support; relies on individual tactical acumen and positioning discipline rather than organized team setups. Operates effectively in breaks without team presence.
terrain_preference: Versatile across challenging one-day terrain (Ardennes finales) and unconventional surfaces (cobbles). Thrives in small group scenarios where positioning and timing trump raw power.
vulnerability: May struggle when forced to initiate attacks against fresher, stronger opponents rather than responding. Tactical approach requires others to expend energy first.
key_patterns: Refuses to work with stronger riders in breaks, conserving energy for final move (2 instances)
Prioritizes positioning discipline over flashy attacking in race finales (2 instances)
Wins from tactical patience rather than dominant physical strength (2 instances)
Effective in small groups where tactical intelligence is decisive (2 instances)
form_trajectory: unknown: 3-year gap between observations makes recent trajectory impossible to assess reliably
